questions.question: Define manifold learning and explain how it differs from linear dimensionality reduction techniques like PCA. Also, mention the fundamental assumption behind manifold learning methods.
keyPoints: Manifold learning assumes data lies on a low-dimensional manifold embedded in high-dimensional space
Unlike PCA (linear), manifold methods can capture non-linear structures
Preserves local neighborhood structure during dimensionality reduction
Works by exploiting geometric properties of the manifold

questions.question: Describe the three main steps of the Locally Linear Embedding (LLE) algorithm and analyze its computational complexity. Also, explain one scenario where LLE may fail.
keyPoints: Step 1: Find k-nearest neighbors for each point
Step 2: Compute reconstruction weights minimizing squared distance
Step 3: Compute low-dimensional embeddings preserving weights
Complexity: O(Dk²N + Dk³) for weight computation; may fail on disconnected manifolds

questions.question: Compare and contrast ISOMAP, Locally Linear Embedding (LLE), and t-SNE. Discuss their mathematical formulations (geodesic distances, local reconstruction, probabilistic distribution), computational complexity, when to use each method, and their respective limitations.
keyPoints: ISOMAP: Approximates geodesic distances using graph shortest paths; MDS on geodesic distances; O(N² log N + N³) for eigenvalue problem
LLE: Preserves local linear structure through reconstruction weights; solves sparse eigenproblem; O(kN² + dN³)
t-SNE: Minimizes KL divergence between Gaussian distributions in high/low dimensions; O(N²) per iteration; best for visualization
Limitations: ISOMAP fails on non-convex manifolds; LLE struggles with outliers; t-SNE not suitable for dimensionality reduction beyond 2-3

questions.question: Derive the optimization objective for Laplacian Eigenmaps. Explain how the graph Laplacian is constructed from the data, and how the eigenvalue problem leads to dimensionality reduction. Discuss the conditions under which this method succeeds and fails, including the choice of kernel width σ.
keyPoints: Objective: Minimize ∑ᵢⱼ Wᵢⱼ||yᵢ - yⱼ||² subject to YᵀDY = I; equivalent to minimizing YᵀLY where L = D - W
Graph construction: Build weighted graph using k-NN or ε-ball; weights from heat kernel Wᵢⱼ = exp(-||xᵢ - xⱼ||²/2σ²)
Eigenvalue problem: Solve Ly = λDy; smallest non-zero eigenvectors form embedding
Succeeds when manifold is well-sampled and connected; fails with poor neighborhood selection or when σ is too small/large
